title: Frankincense room spray
subtitle: A warm, resinous room spray with frankincense, cedarwood, and bergamot
excerpt: Frankincense and cedarwood form the warm, woody base; bergamot lifts the blend with a citrus-floral top note. The vodka disperses the essential oils and acts as a light preservative. One spray covers a standard room for two to three hours.
sourceNotes: Essential oil blending principles: Robert Tisserand and Rodney Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014). Alcohol as a dispersant for essential oils in room sprays: standard perfumery and cosmetic formulation reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each bottle to 250 ml for correct concentration.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Room sprays age well -- the scent often improves after a week as the components meld.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within three months. Discard if the spray develops a flat, medicinal, or unpleasant smell -- citrus top notes (bergamot) oxidise first.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam-distilled or CO2-extracted oil from Boswellia resin. Warm, woody, and resinous -- the classic base note in meditative and ceremonial fragrance blends.

content.text: This spray is built on the resinous depth of

, anchored with cedarwood and brightened with bergamot. The vodka disperses the oils in water and evaporates quickly, leaving only the fragrance. This scent works well in a study, a yoga space, or any room where you want a warm and grounding atmosphere.
Add the essential oils to the 30 ml of vodka and swirl to combine. The alcohol partially dissolves the oils and helps them disperse evenly in the water.

Pour the oil-vodka mixture into the spray bottle. Add 220 ml of distilled water, cap, and shake well. The spray will look cloudy -- this is the oils and water mixing temporarily. It will separate on standing, so shake before each use.

Shake well. Spray two or three times into the centre of the room, directing the mist upward into the air so it falls as a fine cloud rather than landing on surfaces. Avoid spraying directly onto fabrics or wooden furniture. Keep away from eyes. Do not spray around cats.

Keep at room temperature, out of direct sunlight. The amber bottle protects the bergamot from light oxidation. Use within three months. Discard when the bergamot top note has gone flat or the spray smells stale.
